steps.title: Identify the stated consumption and size comparison
steps.content: The passage explicitly states the northern right whale “can consume as much as 24,000 kg krill per day” and is “much larger” than the sei whale (80,000 kg vs. up to 25,000 kg). This direct consumption fact paired with the size difference is the key signal.

steps.title: Match to the answer choices
steps.content:
Choice A directly follows: the larger northern right whale, consuming 24,000 kg daily, eats more krill than the smaller sei whale.

The other three either claim both whales eat the same amount, invent unsupported food scarcity, or contradict that sei whales feed on krill.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
